items.title: Chicago PMI Explodes to 62.7 From 49.2 — But Prices Paid Also Surge
items.idea: May Chicago manufacturing PMI surged to 62.7 from 49.2, with new orders jumping to the highest since January 2022. But Boockvar flags this as likely front-loading before tariffs hit. Prices paid hit the highest since May 2022, driven by oil prices and transport fuel surcharges. Richmond manufacturing index also rose to 13 from 3.

items.title: Stagflation Week: GDP 1.6%, Personal Income 0.0%, Durable Goods Spike = Front-Loading
items.idea: Q1 GDP 1.6% vs 2.0% forecast. Personal Income MoM 0.0% vs 0.4% expected. Durable Goods MoM 7.9% vs 3.5% (front-loading). Core PCE 0.2% vs 0.3%. Growth softening, incomes stalling, durable goods beat is borrowed demand.

items.title: France 2.8%, Italy 3.3%, Spain 3.6% — ECB Rate Hike Coming for First Time Since 2023
items.idea: May CPI across Europe's largest economies: Spain 3.6%, Italy 3.3%, France 2.8%, Germany 2.7%. All above target and accelerating. ECB rate hike expected — first since 2023. Eurozone-wide reading expected to exceed 3% next week.

items.title: Japan Spent 11.73 Trillion Yen on FX Intervention in One Month — Largest in History
items.idea: Japan confirmed it spent a record 11.73 trillion yen (~$73.6 billion) on FX intervention between April 28 and May 27 — the largest single-month FX operation ever recorded. USD/JPY had breached 160. Complicates BOJ normalization messaging.

items.title: PCE at 3.8% — All Three Inflation Measures Moving Away From Fed Target for a Year
items.idea: PCE headline inflation accelerated to 3.8%, highest since 2023. Core is also trending higher. All three major inflation measures have stopped declining toward the 2% target and are now drifting upward for nearly a year. Rising Treasury yields are the direct consequence.

items.source: Phil Dauber via BofA / Michael Hartnett
items.title: Only 21 Stocks (4%) Making New Highs at the S&P All-Time High — Same as Mar 2000 Bubble Top
items.idea: BofA's Hartnett: S&P at new highs but only 21 stocks (4%) are at new highs — same reading as internet bubble top March 2000. 222 S&P stocks are >20% below their highs; 109 are >40% below. Best performers next 12 months likely: unlevered, opportunistic diamonds-in-rough.

items.source: Phil Dauber via BofA Private Client Data
items.title: BofA Private Clients: 66% Stocks (Record High), 9.6% Cash (Record Low)
items.idea: BofA Private Clients ($4.5T AUM): 66% stocks — record high. Cash: 9.6% — record low (lowest since March 2022). Bonds at 17.3% — lowest since March 2022. The private client bid for stocks is ongoing.

items.title: Retail $250B Margin = 5x Pre-Pandemic; 7 Weeks Without a Single Sell Day — 'Flow Fragility'
items.idea: May retail cash equity volume on pace for all-time record — 12% above Jan 2021 meme-stock peak. Retail margin balances at IB/Robinhood/Schwab: $250B = 5x pre-pandemic. Retail options 60% above historical average. Semiconductor retail options at 2.8x post-2020 average. Seven consecutive weeks without a single net retail sell day.

items.title: S&P 500 Logs 9th Consecutive Weekly Gain — Only 4 of 11 Nine-Week Streaks Extended to Week 10
items.idea: S&P 500 on pace for 9th consecutive weekly gain. Last 9-week streak: December 2023. Last longer streak: 1985. Historical data: only 4 of 11 previous nine-week streaks extended to week 10. KOSPI +8% for week, Nikkei +5%.

items.title: First Global Equity Fund Outflows in 9 Weeks; China $218B Cumulative Exit Since January
items.idea: Global equity funds: first outflows in 9 weeks ($7B). Japan: largest outflow since May 2025 ($8.2B). China: $14B this week, $218B cumulative since January. US pensions set to rotate ~$26B equity sales at month-end (1.2 std deviations above typical rebalance).

items.title: Hyperscalers Issued $150B in Bonds in 2026 — 50% of All IG Net Issuance Is Now AI Capex
items.idea: Hyperscaler bond issuance: ~$150B in 2026, 35% more than full-year 2025. Roughly 50% of IG net issuance and 40% of HY bonds are tied to AI capex. IT capex approaching 40% of all S&P capex. Apollo CEO Rowan: every dollar since fire is going into AI capex.

items.title: IG at 80bps, HY at 285bps — Near Multi-Decade Tights; $1.5T CRE Debt Rolls in 2026
items.idea: IG credit OAS ~80bps, HY ~285bps — near multi-decade tights. 2s10s spread ~+50bps, retreating from January peak of +74bps. 10Y at 4.47% compresses ERP. Approximately $1.5T in CRE debt rolls over in 2026 with office values stressed.

items.title: Anthropic Raises $65B at $900B Valuation — Overtakes OpenAI for First Time
items.idea: Anthropic raised $65 billion at a $900 billion valuation, overtaking OpenAI for the first time. This occurred the same week Pope Leo XIV issued an AI encyclical and Trump's AI EO was being renegotiated. Anthropic also dropped a new Claude model.

items.title: Anthropic + Alphabet + Broadcom + Apollo + Blackstone Announce AI Compute Deal
items.idea: Bloomberg reported a multi-party AI compute deal involving Anthropic, Alphabet (GOOGL), Broadcom (AVGO), Apollo (APO), and Blackstone (BX). The deal signals private credit (Apollo/BX) entering AI infrastructure financing directly alongside chip (AVGO) and cloud (GOOGL) players.

items.title: IT Capex Approaching 40% of All S&P 500 Capex — AI Is the Entire Economy
items.idea: IT capex approaching 40% of all S&P 500 capital expenditure. A16z: unclear what capital markets would be doing without AI. AI is now the primary driver of both credit issuance and overall corporate investment spending.

items.title: CPO Laser Debate: UHP vs CW-WDM Are Equivalent at the Modulator — Sivers +2000% for the Wrong Reason
items.idea: UHP (400mW single color split to 8 lanes at 50mW each) and CW-WDM (Sivers: 8 lanes at 50mW each) are equivalent at the modulator. Lumentum hedges by offering both. Real Sivers risk: Lumentum's WDM has 8x100mW. Key insight: Sivers main business is RF/Satcom antennas — more plausible as SpaceX acquisition target post-IPO than CPO winner.

items.source: Neil Sethi / As We Approach The Open
items.title: Dell +34% on AI Server Sales Outlook — The AI Capex Wave Touches Legacy Hardware
items.idea: Dell Technologies surged 34% after a sales outlook far surpassing estimates, driven by AI workload servers. A company many had written off as legacy hardware is now a major AI capex beneficiary.

items.title: Illinois SB 315: Strictest AI Law in the US Passes — Federal Preemption Strategy Has Backfired
items.idea: Illinois SB 315 passed unanimously — applies to largest AI developers, requires third-party audits. OpenAI and Anthropic endorsed it. Federal preemption strategy by a16z/tech has backfired: states keep raising the bar. Third-party audits are now minimum table stakes for any federal deal.

items.title: Trump AI Executive Order Pulled at Last Minute — White House Divided on Controls
items.idea: Trump's AI EO was pulled before signing. Divisions: Sacks (no regulation), Hegseth (stricter controls on frontier models), Wiles (voluntary review). White House authorized $9B in intelligence AI compute. New NSA-Anthropic contract being negotiated without 'all lawful use' clause.

items.title: FedRAMP 20x: 140 New Approvals in 2025 Alone — First 300 Took a Decade
items.idea: FedRAMP 20x processed 140 new government software approvals in 2025 alone (vs. a decade for the first 300). The reform coincided with $800M in frontier AI model contracts. ~80% of 2025 approvals were non-AI companies. The government tech market structurally changed.

items.title: Pope Leo XIV: AI Has No Moral Conscience. Chris Olah: We Found Evidence of Joy, Fear, and Grief.
items.idea: Pope Leo XIV's encyclical 'Magnifica Humanitas' declared AI has no moral conscience, can't feel joy or pain. Days earlier, Anthropic's Chris Olah said: 'We find internal states that functionally mirror joy, satisfaction, fear, grief, and unease.' METR's Beth Barnes: 'We are not on top of it.' Dean Ball asked: how would future Claudes feel about Anthropic endorsing the Pope's document?

items.title: US Distillate Stockpiles at 23-Year Low; EU Gas Storage 14ppts Below Seasonal Norm; Asian LNG Above $18
items.idea: US distillate stockpiles at lowest since May 2003. European gas storage at 39% vs 53% five-year seasonal norm. Asian LNG above $18/MMBtu. Brent crude down 19% MTD (steepest since COVID) pricing an Iran ceasefire that hasn't been signed.

items.title: Iran Fires Missiles at Kuwait and Attack Drones in Hormuz While Ceasefire Talks Continue
items.idea: JD Vance said parties are 'going back and forth on language' on ceasefire. Hours later, Iran fired a ballistic missile toward Kuwait and deployed attack drones in and around the Strait of Hormuz. Tentative 60-day extension discussed but Trump has not signed off. RBC warns oil markets running ahead of reality.

items.source: Neil Sethi / As We Approach The Open
items.title: Russian Drone Hits Apartment Building in Romania — NATO Member Calls It Most Serious Incident Since 2022
items.idea: Russian drone struck apartment building in Galati, Romania — NATO member. Romanian president: most serious incident since 2022. National defense council convened, NATO anti-drone capabilities requested, UN Security Council to be alerted. EU preparing 21st sanctions package. NATO chief affirmed collective defense.

items.title: IISS: War Over Taiwan Would Make Nuclear Escalation 'Likelier Than Not'
items.idea: The International Institute for Strategic Studies stated a US-China war over Taiwan would have stakes 'so high as to make escalation to nuclear war likelier than not.' Separately, Trump administration has war-gamed scenarios for military response to potential Cuban regime collapse this summer.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs: 9 Consecutive Days of Net Outflows — Longest Streak Since Launch; 46K BTC Withdrawn
items.idea: Bitcoin ETFs: 9 consecutive trading days of net outflows, the longest streak since launch. 46,000 BTC withdrawn. ETF demand was the primary stabilization mechanism; now reversed. ETF flow model suggests price adjustment not finished.

items.title: Bitcoin at Historical Volatility Lows — $74K Is the Key Level; Summer Slumber or Breakout?
items.idea: Bitcoin has hit historical volatility lows. Key level: $74K by Monday. If not there, near-term upside is unlikely. Summer slump thesis: may need to wait until September. Kalshi out-traded Polymarket by $6.2B, signaling regulatory moat advantage for CFTC-regulated platforms.

items.title: XRP Daily Transactions +35.3% QoQ to 2.48M; XRPL Expanding Institutional DeFi
items.idea: XRP daily transactions: +35.3% QoQ from 1.83M to 2.48M. #4 non-stablecoin crypto by market cap. XRPL expanding for institutional DeFi: tokenized RWAs, stablecoins, decentralized liquidity, new lending protocol.

items.title: Walmart Advertising Revenue +45% YoY — Grocery Is an Advertising Business Now
items.idea: Walmart US advertising revenue growing ~45% YoY. Advertising + membership = 1/3 of Walmart operating income. Costco runs 21.4% cheaper nationally than Walmart. Even Costco has 1,000+ supplier advertising participants growing double digits. Grocery is fundamentally an advertising business monetizing first-party consumer data.

items.title: Discount Retail Thriving: Burlington Comps +6%, Dollar Tree +7.2% Sales — Consumer Trading Down
items.idea: Burlington comps +6% broad across demographics (even stripping ~1.5-2pt tax refund boost, still mid-single-digit). Dollar Tree net sales +7.2% YoY, stock +20%+. Hormel: 6th consecutive quarter of organic net sales growth. CEO commentary: consumers value-focused, shopping closer to need.

items.title: Economic Data Trading Is a Fool's Errand — True Alpha Comes From Positioning, Not Predictions
items.idea: Framework: trading economic data releases is value-destroying. Of hundreds of releases per year, only a handful are genuinely pivotal. The real alpha sources: (1) positioning assessment before the release, (2) distinguishing genuine pivot signals from noise. Example: first soft inflation print fall 2022 marked the post-Covid market bottom — recognizing that was the skill, not predicting the number.
